subject: docs: add DAISYUI_V5_NOTES cheat sheet and capture audit follow-ups
body:
Adds a project-local DaisyUI v5 reference doc and rolls the full
post-migration audit follow-up pass into SESSION_NOTES / HISTORY /
TODO / AI_MISTAKES / CLAUDE.md.

New file — docs/DAISYUI_V5_NOTES.md:

  Project-local cheat sheet for DaisyUI v5 quirks. Non-exhaustive
  but covers every trap found during the 10-phase migration and
  its follow-up audit. Sections:

  - "v4 -> v5 removed modifiers" table: input-bordered,
    select-bordered, textarea-bordered, btn-bordered, form-control,
    input-group, card-bordered, card-compact, card-side, btn-group,
    tab-bordered, tab-lifted, menu-compact, menu-normal, mask-*.
    Each row has the v4 name and the v5 replacement with notes.
  - "Verification recipe": grep recipe for enumerating what v5
    actually ships in the built CSS (run after every build that
    introduces a new DaisyUI component modifier). Narrower
    per-component grep example also included.
  - "Project conventions we use": concrete className templates
    for cards, buttons, badges, alerts, modals, toasts, tabs,
    form inputs, and checkboxes as we standardized on them in
    the 10-phase migration.
  - "Deliberately NOT used": dropdown, menu, collapse, drawer —
    each with the rejection reason so future audits don't re-open
    the question.
  - "Theme registration": pointer to the scripts/theme-config.js
    propagator flow.
  - "Related docs" cross-reference list.

docs/AI_MISTAKES.md:

  The 2026-04-13 entry (-bordered trap post-mortem) previously
  ended with a TODO pointing at "docs/implementations/
  DAISYUI_V5_NOTES.md" as a future home for a cheat sheet. The
  implementations/ subdirectory doesn't exist in this repo —
  CLAUDE.md's Suggested Implementations section explicitly
  forbids creating local copies of implementation patterns.
  Rewritten to point at the new top-level docs/DAISYUI_V5_NOTES.md
  instead, and the placeholder TODO is removed.

docs/HISTORY.md:

  New "2026-04-13 Post-migration audit" section ABOVE the 10-phase
  sweep entry. Documents all four follow-up fixes with:
    1. -bordered v4 cruft removal (commits de9bd4f + 9e2b154, already
       in history but now cross-referenced from the audit section).
    2. stat-card dead-wrapper merge in Summary.jsx (new).
    3. Toast nested aria-live double-announce fix (new).
    4. HamburgerMenu React Portal for stacking context (new).
  Two remaining follow-ups (Playwright smoke test + visual
  regression baselines) listed with rationale for why they're
  deferred to the test-infrastructure TODO.

docs/SESSION_NOTES.md:

  New "2026-04-13 post-migration audit follow-up" block at the top
  of Current State, ABOVE the prior 10-phase block. Numbered list
  of the four fixes with a sentence explaining each, plus a note
  on the two test-infrastructure TODOs and why they're pending.

docs/TODO.md:

  - Removed the "Stacking Context" entry (fixed in commit 1f71c6d
    via React Portal).
  - New "Test infrastructure (post-migration follow-up)" section
    with two items: browser-runtime Playwright smoke test for the
    DaisyUI migration checklist, and visual regression baselines
    (per tab x per theme = 8 baselines). Each item describes what
    it should check and why it's deferred.
  - Footer "Last updated" paragraph rewritten to mention the four
    follow-up fixes alongside the 10-phase sweep.

CLAUDE.md:

  The documentation table now lists docs/DAISYUI_V5_NOTES.md with
  its update trigger ("when encountering a new DaisyUI v5 quirk,
  or when adding a new component class to JSX") so future sessions
  know to consult and maintain the cheat sheet.

Tags: documentation, daisyui, migration, audit, postmortem
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: daisyui-component-migration
Semver: patch
title: add DAISYUI_V5_NOTES cheat sheet and capture audit follow-ups
